data.title: Answering Service Care: Never Miss a Client Call Again
data.excerpt: Discover why Answering Service Care's 24/7 live agents handle your calls professionally. Plans from $40/mo, no contracts. See if it fits your business today.

$media.alt: Answering Service Care: Never Miss a Client Call Again

children.text: *Disclosure: This post contains affiliate links. If you sign up through our links, we may earn a commission at no extra cost to you.*

You know that sinking feeling when you check your phone after a meeting and see three missed calls from unknown numbers? One of them was probably a new client ready to hire you. The other two? Could be referrals, partnerships, or repeat customers who needed something urgent.

Missed calls cost real money. Not in some abstract, theoretical way—literally. A study by Forbes found that 85% of people who can't reach a business on the first try won't call back. They just move on to the next option on Google.
That's exactly why
Answering Service Care
exists, and why I think it's one of the smartest investments a small business owner can make this year.
What Answering Service Care Actually Does
Let me break it down without the marketing fluff.

Answering Service Care is a US-based answering service that provides real human agents to pick up your business phone calls 24 hours a day, 7 days a week. These aren't robots reading scripts. They're trained professionals who answer in your company's name, follow your custom instructions, take messages, transfer calls, and even handle basic customer questions.
Here's the simple version: when you can't answer—because you're with a client, driving, sleeping, or just need a break—someone else does. And they do it well.

I've tested a handful of answering services over the years, and what struck me about Answering Service Care is how seriously they take the "care" part of their name. The onboarding process walks you through exactly how you want calls handled. You define the greeting. You set the rules for which calls get transferred and which get a message taken. You choose what information to collect from callers.
The result? Callers don't feel like they reached a call center. They feel like they reached your office.
If you want a deeper walkthrough, we published a full
step-by-step guide on how to use Answering Service Care
that covers setup from scratch.
Why I Keep Recommending It to Business Owners

I talk to a lot of entrepreneurs. Consultants, agency owners, contractors, medical practices, law firms, e-commerce brands—you name it. And the conversation almost always hits the same wall: "I know I'm missing calls, but I can't afford a full-time receptionist."
A full-time receptionist costs $30,000-$45,000 a year in the US. Answering Service Care starts at $40 a month.
That's not a typo. Forty dollars.
Check their current plans and pricing here

and you'll see exactly what I mean. The Starter Plan gives you 24/7 coverage, one phone number, call transfers, email and SMS notifications, custom hold music, personalized greetings, and access to their portal and mobile app. All for less than most people spend on coffee in a week.
But the value goes beyond just picking up the phone. Here's what actually moves the needle for businesses:
You look professional, instantly.

Whether you're a solo freelancer or a five-person shop, a dedicated answering line with custom greetings makes you sound like a 50-person operation. First impressions matter, and a live voice beats voicemail every single time.
You capture leads while you sleep.
I mean this literally. Their agents work around the clock, so the person who calls at 11 PM because they found your website while browsing? That's a captured lead instead of a lost one.
You reclaim your focus.
When you know every call is being handled properly, you stop interrupting deep work to check your phone. That alone is worth the monthly cost.
For a deeper look at real-world scenarios, our guide on
Answering Service Care use cases
covers how different industries are putting it to work.
New and Standout Features Worth Knowing About
Answering Service Care isn't one of those "set it and forget it" tools that never updates. They've been actively expanding their feature set, and a few additions caught my attention.
The ASC Mobile App.

This is a game-changer for anyone who runs their business from their phone. The app lets you access messages, manage call activity, update your availability, and stay in full control of your service from anywhere. You can review call logs, read transcripts, and adjust settings without opening a laptop. For mobile-first business owners, this eliminates the friction that makes some people hesitant about answering services.
Live Chat Integration.

Not every customer wants to call. The Small Business Plan and above include live chat support, which means your website visitors can connect with a real agent through chat too. It's an additional touchpoint that captures people who'd otherwise bounce off your site.
Outbound Calling.

This is newer and honestly underappreciated. Beyond inbound calls, Answering Service Care can handle outbound calling on your behalf—appointment confirmations, follow-ups, lead callbacks. Think of it as having a part-time admin assistant without the payroll commitment.
Live Multilingual Support.

Available at $1 per additional minute, this feature lets you serve callers in multiple languages. If your customer base includes Spanish speakers, for example, you can have agents switch seamlessly. In a country where over 41 million people speak Spanish at home, that's not a nice-to-have—it's a competitive advantage.
Test Your Account Feature.

They built a straightforward way to place a test call and experience your answering service exactly as a caller would. You can review messages, check call handling, and fine-tune everything before going live. It removes the anxiety of wondering whether things are working.
We've covered all the
latest Answering Service Care features
in detail if you want to dig deeper into what's new.
How the Pricing Actually Works
I appreciate transparent pricing. No one wants to sit through a sales demo just to find out what something costs. Answering Service Care puts their plans front and center.
Starter Plan — $40/month.
Zero included minutes, $1.75 per additional minute. This is perfect if you get occasional calls and just need a safety net.
Small Business Plan — $179/month.
100 included minutes, $1.70 per additional minute. Adds a Follow Me number, live chat at $5 per chat, and a dedicated account manager. This is where most small businesses land.
Pro Plan — $329/month.
More included minutes, lower per-minute rates, and the full feature suite. Best for businesses with consistent call volume.
A few things I genuinely respect about their pricing model:
No long-term contracts.
You can cancel anytime. They earn your business month after month instead of locking you in.
Annual discount.
Pay upfront yearly and save up to 10%. That's real savings on a plan you'd be using anyway.
Transparent per-minute rates.
You know exactly what you're paying. No hidden fees, no surprise charges at the end of the month.
The cost-per-call math works out favorably compared to hiring even a part-time employee. And unlike an employee, the service never calls in sick, never takes vacation, and covers holidays.
For tips on getting the most value from your plan, check out our
Answering Service Care tips and best practices
Who Is Answering Service Care Ideal For?
Not every tool is for everyone, and I'd rather be honest about that. Here's who I think gets the most out of this service:
Solo professionals and consultants.

You're the product. Every hour you spend answering routine phone calls is an hour you're not billing. Answering Service Care acts as a buffer that lets you focus on revenue-generating work while making sure no opportunity slips through.
Small businesses without a receptionist.
If you have 2-15 employees and everyone is too busy to answer calls consistently, this fills the gap at a fraction of the cost of hiring someone.
Service-based businesses with after-hours demand.

Plumbers, HVAC techs, lawyers, property managers—industries where emergencies happen at 2 AM. Having a live voice answer instead of voicemail can be the difference between winning and losing a client.
E-commerce brands scaling up customer support.
When your inbox and chat volume outgrow what your team can handle, adding a phone line with professional agents prevents customer churn.
Growing companies testing new markets.
If you're expanding to a new city or launching a new product line, an answering service lets you establish a local presence without opening a physical office.
Anyone who works with clients who expect to reach a human.
Some industries just can't get away with voicemail. Medical practices, law offices, financial advisors, real estate agents—your clients expect responsiveness, and Answering Service Care delivers it.
Honestly, if your business receives phone calls that matter—and almost every business does—there's a version of this service that makes sense for you.
Ready to try it? Start with their Starter Plan
and see how it feels to never miss another call.
How It Fits Into Your Existing Workflow
One concern I hear from business owners is whether adding an answering service creates more work. The opposite is actually true.

Answering Service Care integrates smoothly with how you already operate. Messages arrive via email, SMS, or through the ASC portal and mobile app. You don't need to learn new software or change your daily routine.
If you're already using project management tools like
ClickUp

, you can forward call summaries directly into your task list. Client calls that need follow-up become action items automatically. Pair it with your CRM, and suddenly every incoming call is logged, categorized, and trackable.

The dedicated account manager (included in the Small Business Plan and above) acts as your single point of contact. Instead of explaining your preferences to a different person every time, you work with someone who knows your business and can fine-tune your call handling as your needs evolve.
The Bottom Line

I've recommended Answering Service Care to multiple people in my network, and the feedback has been consistently positive. The combination of real human agents, US-based operations, transparent pricing, and zero contracts makes it one of the most accessible professional services available to small businesses.
You don't need a massive call volume to justify it. You just need to care about the calls you're currently missing.
Every missed call is a missed relationship. A missed invoice. A missed referral. Answering Service Care makes sure those moments become connections instead of regrets.
Get started with Answering Service Care today
and experience the difference a professional answering service makes for your business.

children.text: How much does Answering Service Care cost?

Plans start at $40/month for the Starter Plan with no included minutes and $1.75 per additional minute. The Small Business Plan is $179/month with 100 included minutes, and the Pro Plan is $329/month. Annual billing saves up to 10%. There are no long-term contracts.
Are the answering agents real people or AI?

Answering Service Care uses real, US-based human agents. They answer calls in your company's name, follow your custom scripts, take messages, transfer calls, and handle customer questions with professional, personalized service.
Can I try Answering Service Care before committing?

Yes. They offer a built-in test call feature so you can experience your answering service exactly as a caller would before going live. Since there are no long-term contracts, you can also cancel anytime if it's not the right fit.
Does Answering Service Care support multiple languages?

Yes. Live multilingual support is available at $1 per additional minute, allowing agents to assist callers in languages like Spanish. This is especially valuable for businesses serving diverse communities.
